findings.title: Ollama cache race condition on concurrent /api/config requests
findings.issue: Two concurrent /api/config requests arriving when cache is expired could both trigger Ollama probe simultaneously. Object assignment is atomic in V8 so no data corruption, but produces redundant network calls.
findings.suggestion: Use a pending promise to coalesce concurrent probes. Store in-flight promise and return it for subsequent callers.
findings.impact: Wasteful duplicate HTTP probes to Ollama. No functional impact for a local-only tool.

findings.title: Error messages may leak internal file paths to client
findings.issue: Some catch blocks pass raw error messages (including ENOENT file paths) directly to the API response. An attacker with local network access could learn filesystem structure.
findings.suggestion: Wrap catch blocks at API boundary to sanitize file paths from error messages before sending to client.
findings.impact: Information disclosure of file paths. Minimal risk since server only binds to localhost and CORS is restricted.
